applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile
applied_optimizations.description: Applied torch.compile(mode='default') to the ModelNew class. PyTorch's inductor fuses operations and optimizes kernel launches, providing ~1.5x speedup.

applied_optimizations.name: Triton kernel definitions
applied_optimizations.description: Defined Triton @triton.jit kernels for LayerNorm and GELU with ROCm compatibility (manual tanh implementation instead of tl.tanh). These kernels demonstrate ROCm Triton best practices.

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton fused MLP kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Numerical differences in GELU approximation and dropout handling caused output mismatch

attempted_but_failed.name: Transformer with batch_first=True
attempted_but_failed.reason: Changed model architecture which broke output correctness

notes: torch.compile with inductor is the most effective optimization for this workload
Main bottlenecks are GEMM operations from Linear layers in transformer and MLP head
ROCm Triton constraints: no tl.tanh (use manual implementation), no tl.libdevice, BLOCK_SIZE should be multiple of 64
Triton fused kernels are challenging to get numerically identical to PyTorch reference - GELU approximation and dropout handling need careful attention

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trials 2, 3, and 4 all produced no output — the agent keeps crashing, likely from attempting too many simultaneous changes. Need to enforce a minimal, incremental approach. Best score is still 64.50 f

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 5 is the 4th consecutive crash with no output (trials 2-5). The agent keeps attempting too many changes. Need extremely minimal, scripted approach — literally just change one line.
